lanes.title: Marking and identity detail
lanes.request_prompt: Capture readable close-ups of every IC marking, connector label, regulator marking, crystal, and silkscreen model text.

lanes.title: Layout geometry anchors
lanes.request_prompt: Add an overview photo or annotated crop with normalized boxes for unmapped evidence items.

lanes.title: Cross-view confirmation
lanes.request_prompt: Recapture the main components/connectors from another angle so single-photo claims can be cross-checked.

lanes.title: Backside or hidden-area context
lanes.request_prompt: If accessible, capture the backside, solder side, cable underside, or hidden area so hidden routes and damage are not silently assumed.

capture_coverage.claim_boundary: Capture coverage grades visual observability only; it cannot prove pinout, nets, voltage, or safe reuse.

reconstruction_summary.source_policy: Repeated visual observations raise candidate confidence but do not prove pinout, nets, voltage, or safe reuse.

next_capture_requests.prompt: Capture readable close-ups of every IC marking, connector label, regulator marking, crystal, and silkscreen model text.
Add an overview photo or annotated crop with normalized boxes for unmapped evidence items.
Recapture the main components/connectors from another angle so single-photo claims can be cross-checked.
If accessible, capture the backside, solder side, cable underside, or hidden area so hidden routes and damage are not silently assumed.
Add more board photos from different distances or angles.
Capture a wider photo that includes readable markings and their surrounding component context.
Add an overview photo or annotated crop with normalized boxes for unmapped evidence items.

hardware_plan.goal: Salvage and reuse useful low-voltage functions from this physical board; produce next bench actions before any splice or power authority.

required_measurements: Record passing ground/reference continuity and connector continuity evidence.
Record passing current draw/current-limit evidence during first power.
Record passing unpowered resistance/no-short evidence between power and ground.
Record passing thermal behavior after first power and output-function test.
Record passing voltage, polarity, and logic-level evidence under current limit.
Attach measured topology or continuity-backed pinout evidence before first power/splice.
blockers: Missing measurement categories: continuity, current, resistance, thermal, voltage.
Missing trusted measurement provenance: continuity, current, resistance, thermal, voltage.
Image-only evidence cannot authorize repair, power, or splice.
authority.claim_boundary: Hardware plan is advisory until repair authority, resource evidence gates, and first-power measurements close.

outcome_memory.learning_policy: negative or unsafe outcomes gate matching selected resources; positive outcomes add context but do not bypass evidence gates

Add an overview or annotated photo that maps unmapped connector/component evidence into normalized board coordinates.
Map 40-pin GPIO header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 40-pin GPIO header.

Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-A port 1 to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
Map USB-A port 2: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.

Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-C Power In to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.

items.summary: Visual board candidates exist without measured topology.
evidence: Vision evidence can propose components and connectors but cannot prove pinout.
items.resolution: Capture measured connector pinout, no-short, ground, and voltage evidence.

steps.action: Attach measured connector pinout/topology evidence with no unknown pins.
steps.expected_result: Every used connector pin has a measured role and voltage/domain where relevant.
steps.fail_branch: Measured pinout is complete.

steps.action: Record passing trusted resistance evidence.
steps.expected_result: No power rail reads as a dead short to ground.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing resistance evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted continuity evidence.
steps.expected_result: Ground/reference continuity is stable.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing continuity evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted voltage evidence.
steps.expected_result: Voltage and polarity match the measured low-voltage domain.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing voltage evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted current evidence.
steps.expected_result: Current draw remains within the configured current limit.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing current evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted thermal evidence.
steps.expected_result: Thermal behavior remains normal through the function test.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing thermal evidence.

steps.action: Record successful first power, thermal behavior, and output_function_verified=true.
steps.expected_result: Successful output function, first power, and thermal outcome are recorded.
steps.fail_branch: Terminal successful outcome is recorded.

steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Measure unpowered resistance between every power rail and ground.
steps.expected_result: No rail reads as a dead short unless the design explicitly expects it.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; isolate the shorted rail before power.

steps.action: Power through a current limit and record input voltage, rail voltage, and polarity.
steps.expected_result: Rails match the measured or expected low-voltage domain.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; inspect regulator/input protection path.

steps.action: Record thermal behavior during first-power and function test.
steps.expected_result: No abnormal hot spot, smoke, odor, or thermal runaway.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; isolate load, regulator, or shorted component.

bench_protocol_pack.title: Unknown low-voltage board baseline protocol
required_equipment: current-limited bench supply or protected USB source
setup_controls: Keep unknown boards unpowered until no-short and polarity checks pass.
Use current limits for every first-power or load test.
Record instrument, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI for each passing measurement.
Stop on smoke, odor, abnormal heat, unexpected current, shorted rail, or unstable output.
For BCM2711, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For SBC, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For RASPBERRY PI, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.

steps.action: Photograph front/back, labels, connectors, and damage before testing.
steps.expected_result: Visual record is complete.

steps.action: Measure every obvious power rail or connector power pin to ground.
steps.expected_result: No dead short.

steps.action: Map connector ground/reference and likely power pins.
steps.expected_result: Baseline connector roles are known.

steps.action: Power only through a current limit after no-short passes.
steps.expected_result: Voltage/current behavior is bounded.

steps.action: Observe thermal behavior during first-power.
steps.expected_result: No abnormal hot spot.

acceptance: Board identity is narrowed to a reusable function.
No-short, pinout, voltage/current, and thermal evidence pass.
Terminal function outcome is recorded before release.

release_artifacts_required: production release manifest for any release claim
bench_protocol_pack.release_boundary: This pack defines what must be proven for a scoped low-voltage release. It cannot authorize battery, mains, high-voltage, laser, or unbounded reuse without a separate specialist authority.

candidates.label: USB/serial bridge, connector, boot, or logic-level fault
diagnostic_actions: Run logic-level, shared-ground, loopback, and safe serial-capture steps.

candidates.label: Sensor front-end, bus, pullup, or connector fault
diagnostic_actions: Verify supply, pullups, bus idle, low-speed scan, and connector continuity.

candidates.label: Display/UI pin map, current limit, or connector fault
diagnostic_actions: Map pins, verify current limits, and test through protected controller I/O.

candidates.label: Audio load, amplifier, connector, or protection fault
diagnostic_actions: Verify load impedance, idle current, low-level output, and thermal behavior.

salvage_value_decision.rationale: Visual evidence can identify candidates, but value depends on measured function.
salvage_value_decision.recommended_exit: Collect baseline measurements before spending parts or time.
salvage_value_decision.claim_boundary: Economic estimate is heuristic until a successful function outcome is recorded.

items.summary: Visual board candidates exist without measured topology.
evidence: Vision evidence can propose components and connectors but cannot prove pinout.
items.resolution: Capture measured connector pinout, no-short, ground, and voltage evidence.

steps.action: Attach measured connector pinout/topology evidence with no unknown pins.
steps.expected_result: Every used connector pin has a measured role and voltage/domain where relevant.
steps.fail_branch: Measured pinout is complete.

steps.action: Record passing trusted resistance evidence.
steps.expected_result: No power rail reads as a dead short to ground.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing resistance evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted continuity evidence.
steps.expected_result: Ground/reference continuity is stable.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing continuity evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted voltage evidence.
steps.expected_result: Voltage and polarity match the measured low-voltage domain.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing voltage evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted current evidence.
steps.expected_result: Current draw remains within the configured current limit.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing current evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted thermal evidence.
steps.expected_result: Thermal behavior remains normal through the function test.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing thermal evidence.

steps.action: Record successful first power, thermal behavior, and output_function_verified=true.
steps.expected_result: Successful output function, first power, and thermal outcome are recorded.
steps.fail_branch: Terminal successful outcome is recorded.

steps.action: Record passing ground/reference continuity and connector continuity evidence.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing current draw/current-limit evidence during first power.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing unpowered resistance/no-short evidence between power and ground.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing thermal behavior after first power and output-function test.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing voltage, polarity, and logic-level evidence under current limit.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Measure unpowered resistance between every power rail and ground.
steps.expected_result: No rail reads as a dead short unless the design explicitly expects it.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; isolate the shorted rail before power.

steps.action: Power through a current limit and record input voltage, rail voltage, and polarity.
steps.expected_result: Rails match the measured or expected low-voltage domain.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; inspect regulator/input protection path.

steps.action: Record thermal behavior during first-power and function test.
steps.expected_result: No abnormal hot spot, smoke, odor, or thermal runaway.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; isolate load, regulator, or shorted component.

steps.action: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
steps.expected_result: Measured topology_evidence.v1 row is attached, or a negative result is recorded explicitly.
steps.fail_branch: Keep the candidate visual topology outside the splice/power trust boundary.

candidates.label: USB/serial bridge, connector, boot, or logic-level fault
diagnostic_actions: Run logic-level, shared-ground, loopback, and safe serial-capture steps.

candidates.label: Sensor front-end, bus, pullup, or connector fault
diagnostic_actions: Verify supply, pullups, bus idle, low-speed scan, and connector continuity.

candidates.label: Display/UI pin map, current limit, or connector fault
diagnostic_actions: Map pins, verify current limits, and test through protected controller I/O.

candidates.label: Audio load, amplifier, connector, or protection fault
diagnostic_actions: Verify load impedance, idle current, low-level output, and thermal behavior.

bench_protocol_pack.title: Unknown low-voltage board baseline protocol
required_equipment: current-limited bench supply or protected USB source
setup_controls: Keep unknown boards unpowered until no-short and polarity checks pass.
Use current limits for every first-power or load test.
Record instrument, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI for each passing measurement.
Stop on smoke, odor, abnormal heat, unexpected current, shorted rail, or unstable output.
For BCM2711, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For SBC, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For RASPBERRY PI, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.

steps.action: Photograph front/back, labels, connectors, and damage before testing.
steps.expected_result: Visual record is complete.

steps.action: Measure every obvious power rail or connector power pin to ground.
steps.expected_result: No dead short.

steps.action: Map connector ground/reference and likely power pins.
steps.expected_result: Baseline connector roles are known.

steps.action: Power only through a current limit after no-short passes.
steps.expected_result: Voltage/current behavior is bounded.

steps.action: Observe thermal behavior during first-power.
steps.expected_result: No abnormal hot spot.

acceptance: Board identity is narrowed to a reusable function.
No-short, pinout, voltage/current, and thermal evidence pass.
Terminal function outcome is recorded before release.

release_artifacts_required: production release manifest for any release claim
bench_protocol_pack.release_boundary: This pack defines what must be proven for a scoped low-voltage release. It cannot authorize battery, mains, high-voltage, laser, or unbounded reuse without a separate specialist authority.

closure_lanes.title: Cross-view visual coverage
closure_lanes.objective: Get enough non-rigid photo observations to cross-check markings, connectors, damage, and placement.

tasks.prompt: Add an overview photo or annotated crop with normalized boxes for unmapped evidence items.
Add more board photos from different distances or angles.
Capture a wider photo that includes readable markings and their surrounding component context.
Capture readable close-ups of every IC marking, connector label, regulator marking, crystal, and silkscreen model text.
Capture several non-identical board observations: whole-board overview with scale, angled view, connector edge, and any backside/hidden area that exists.
If accessible, capture the backside, solder side, cable underside, or hidden area so hidden routes and damage are not silently assumed.
Recapture the main components/connectors from another angle so single-photo claims can be cross-checked.
Capture a damage and safety review pass: battery areas, hot spots, corrosion, burns, cracked parts, bodge wires, and power input path.

closure_lanes.title: Part and product identity
closure_lanes.objective: Ground board function claims in visible markings, known packages, public pinouts, or datasheets.

closure_lanes.title: Measured topology and pinout
closure_lanes.objective: Turn visual/reference topology into measured connector, pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, and thermal evidence.

tasks.prompt: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
Map 40-pin GPIO header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map Ethernet RJ45: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map HDMI: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map USB-A port 1: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 40-pin GPIO header.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on Ethernet RJ45.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on HDMI.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-A port 1.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for 40-pin GPIO header before any external connection.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-A port 1 before any external connection.
Record connector ground to exposed ground. Confirm common ground reference.
Record current draw under current-limited supply. Record first-power current at a safe current limit.
Record input voltage and polarity. Measure supply rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Record logic high voltage. Measure the reused signal voltage domain.
Record power to ground no-short. Unpowered resistance check between supply and ground.
Record thermal behavior after first power. Record no abnormal heating after first power.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) ground reference continuity: 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) logic voltage domain: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Measure idle/high logic level before connecting to another controller or adapter.
Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) signal pin continuity map: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Map each reusable signal to a labeled pad, connector, or target net before splicing.
Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) supply voltage and polarity: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) supply-to-ground no-short: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3 vs 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.

Record Ethernet RJ45 (ethernet) high-speed/shield reference check: 1:BI_DA+, 2:BI_DA-, 3:BI_DB+, 4:BI_DC+, 5:BI_DC-, 6:BI_DB-, 7:BI_DD+, 8:BI_DD-. Treat high-speed pairs as reference-only unless connector, shield, and protection path are confirmed.
Record Ethernet RJ45 (ethernet) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record HDMI (hdmip) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record HDMI (hdmip) supply-to-ground no-short: 18:+5V vs 17:DDC_GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.
Add an overview or annotated photo that maps unmapped connector/component evidence into normalized board coordinates.

resource_interpretation.core_rule: salvage, owned inventory, procurement, and designed blocks are scored as resources; the resource pool changes, not the engine.
resource_interpretation.constrained: Prefer already-owned or salvaged resources and surface evidence gates before reuse.
resource_interpretation.open_procurement: Prefer reliable procurable modules when they beat uncertain reuse.
resource_interpretation.hybrid: Reuse proven resources and buy only the gaps.

mount moving parts so wires cannot touch blades, gears, belts, rails, or hot surfaces
label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification

battery or mains hazards are cleared

next_steps.expected_result: Every used connector pin has a measured role and voltage/domain where relevant.
No power rail reads as a dead short to ground.
Ground/reference continuity is stable.
Voltage and polarity match the measured low-voltage domain.
Current draw remains within the configured current limit.
Thermal behavior remains normal through the function test.

bench_protocol_pack.title: Unknown low-voltage board baseline protocol
required_equipment: current-limited bench supply or protected USB source
setup_controls: Keep unknown boards unpowered until no-short and polarity checks pass.
Use current limits for every first-power or load test.
Record instrument, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI for each passing measurement.
Stop on smoke, odor, abnormal heat, unexpected current, shorted rail, or unstable output.
For BCM2711, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For SBC, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For RASPBERRY PI, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.

bench_protocol_pack.title: Unknown low-voltage board baseline protocol
required_equipment: current-limited bench supply or protected USB source
setup_controls: Keep unknown boards unpowered until no-short and polarity checks pass.
Use current limits for every first-power or load test.
Record instrument, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI for each passing measurement.
Stop on smoke, odor, abnormal heat, unexpected current, shorted rail, or unstable output.
For BCM2711, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For SBC, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For RASPBERRY PI, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.

evidence: Vision evidence can propose components and connectors but cannot prove pinout.

steps.action: Attach measured connector pinout/topology evidence with no unknown pins.
steps.expected_result: Every used connector pin has a measured role and voltage/domain where relevant.
steps.fail_branch: Measured pinout is complete.

steps.action: Record passing trusted resistance evidence.
steps.expected_result: No power rail reads as a dead short to ground.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing resistance evidence.

steps.fail_branch: Missing passing continuity evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted voltage evidence.
steps.expected_result: Voltage and polarity match the measured low-voltage domain.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing voltage evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted current evidence.
steps.expected_result: Current draw remains within the configured current limit.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing current evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted thermal evidence.
steps.expected_result: Thermal behavior remains normal through the function test.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing thermal evidence.

steps.fail_branch: Terminal successful outcome is recorded.

steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Measure unpowered resistance between every power rail and ground.

steps.fail_branch: Stop; isolate the shorted rail before power.

steps.action: Record thermal behavior during first-power and function test.
steps.expected_result: No abnormal hot spot, smoke, odor, or thermal runaway.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; isolate load, regulator, or shorted component.

bench_protocol_pack.title: Unknown low-voltage board baseline protocol
required_equipment: current-limited bench supply or protected USB source
setup_controls: Keep unknown boards unpowered until no-short and polarity checks pass.
Use current limits for every first-power or load test.
Record instrument, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI for each passing measurement.
Stop on smoke, odor, abnormal heat, unexpected current, shorted rail, or unstable output.
For BCM2711, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For SBC, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For RASPBERRY PI, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.

steps.action: Photograph front/back, labels, connectors, and damage before testing.
steps.expected_result: Visual record is complete.

steps.action: Measure every obvious power rail or connector power pin to ground.
steps.expected_result: No dead short.

steps.action: Map connector ground/reference and likely power pins.
steps.expected_result: Baseline connector roles are known.

steps.action: Power only through a current limit after no-short passes.
steps.expected_result: Voltage/current behavior is bounded.

steps.action: Observe thermal behavior during first-power.
steps.expected_result: No abnormal hot spot.

acceptance: Board identity is narrowed to a reusable function.
No-short, pinout, voltage/current, and thermal evidence pass.
Terminal function outcome is recorded before release.

release_artifacts_required: production release manifest for any release claim
bench_protocol_pack.release_boundary: This pack defines what must be proven for a scoped low-voltage release. It cannot authorize battery, mains, high-voltage, laser, or unbounded reuse without a separate specialist authority.

candidates.label: USB/serial bridge, connector, boot, or logic-level fault
diagnostic_actions: Run logic-level, shared-ground, loopback, and safe serial-capture steps.

candidates.label: Sensor front-end, bus, pullup, or connector fault
diagnostic_actions: Verify supply, pullups, bus idle, low-speed scan, and connector continuity.

candidates.label: Display/UI pin map, current limit, or connector fault
diagnostic_actions: Map pins, verify current limits, and test through protected controller I/O.

candidates.label: Audio load, amplifier, connector, or protection fault
diagnostic_actions: Verify load impedance, idle current, low-level output, and thermal behavior.

salvage_value_decision.rationale: Visual evidence can identify candidates, but value depends on measured function.
salvage_value_decision.recommended_exit: Collect baseline measurements before spending parts or time.
salvage_value_decision.claim_boundary: Economic estimate is heuristic until a successful function outcome is recorded.

allowed_layout_actions: whole-board reuse after authority gates close
connector-based harness reuse after measured pinout
label and fixture known connector entry points
prohibited_layout_actions: board-section cutting from visual evidence alone
hidden-trace splice or copper cut without backside/topology proof
missing_layout_evidence: Attach additional angle/side/closeup photos before any section salvage or trace-cut claim.
Attach component/connector geometry or annotated regions for layout-aware salvage.
Attach measured pinout/topology before section salvage or external splice.
layout_reuse_boundaries.claim_boundary: Layout boundaries are conservative. Without front/back geometry and measured topology, the engine may recommend whole-board or connector reuse, but not board-section cutting or hidden-trace salvage.

reuse_splice_strategy.strategy_summary: treat the board as unknown until measured; readiness=visual_mapping_only

recipes.title: Measure first, then decide reuse versus harvest
splice_steps: Run no-short, voltage, current, and thermal checks.
acceptance: function is identified and measured
no hard safety blocker remains
prohibitions: Do not power or splice unknown boards from visual evidence alone.

allowed_actions: Capture markings, connector close-ups, and baseline continuity/no-short measurements.
Prepare candidate splice recipes but keep them gated.
reuse_splice_strategy.best_next_checkpoint: Attach measured connector pinout/topology evidence with no unknown pins.
reuse_splice_strategy.claim_boundary: Splice recipes are templates until connector pinout, voltage/current limits, and terminal outcome evidence are recorded.

bench_protocol_pack.title: Unknown low-voltage board baseline protocol
required_equipment: current-limited bench supply or protected USB source
setup_controls: Keep unknown boards unpowered until no-short and polarity checks pass.
Use current limits for every first-power or load test.
Record instrument, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI for each passing measurement.
Stop on smoke, odor, abnormal heat, unexpected current, shorted rail, or unstable output.
For BCM2711, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For SBC, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For RASPBERRY PI, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.

steps.action: Photograph front/back, labels, connectors, and damage before testing.
steps.expected_result: Visual record is complete.

steps.action: Measure every obvious power rail or connector power pin to ground.
steps.expected_result: No dead short.

steps.action: Map connector ground/reference and likely power pins.
steps.expected_result: Baseline connector roles are known.

steps.action: Power only through a current limit after no-short passes.
steps.expected_result: Voltage/current behavior is bounded.

steps.action: Observe thermal behavior during first-power.
steps.expected_result: No abnormal hot spot.

acceptance: Board identity is narrowed to a reusable function.
No-short, pinout, voltage/current, and thermal evidence pass.
Terminal function outcome is recorded before release.

release_artifacts_required: production release manifest for any release claim
bench_protocol_pack.release_boundary: This pack defines what must be proven for a scoped low-voltage release. It cannot authorize battery, mains, high-voltage, laser, or unbounded reuse without a separate specialist authority.

candidates.label: USB/serial bridge, connector, boot, or logic-level fault
diagnostic_actions: Run logic-level, shared-ground, loopback, and safe serial-capture steps.

candidates.label: Sensor front-end, bus, pullup, or connector fault
diagnostic_actions: Verify supply, pullups, bus idle, low-speed scan, and connector continuity.

candidates.label: Display/UI pin map, current limit, or connector fault
diagnostic_actions: Map pins, verify current limits, and test through protected controller I/O.

candidates.label: Audio load, amplifier, connector, or protection fault
diagnostic_actions: Verify load impedance, idle current, low-level output, and thermal behavior.

salvage_value_decision.rationale: Visual evidence can identify candidates, but value depends on measured function.
salvage_value_decision.recommended_exit: Collect baseline measurements before spending parts or time.
salvage_value_decision.claim_boundary: Economic estimate is heuristic until a successful function outcome is recorded.

allowed_layout_actions: whole-board reuse after authority gates close
connector-based harness reuse after measured pinout
label and fixture known connector entry points
prohibited_layout_actions: board-section cutting from visual evidence alone
hidden-trace splice or copper cut without backside/topology proof
missing_layout_evidence: Attach additional angle/side/closeup photos before any section salvage or trace-cut claim.
Attach component/connector geometry or annotated regions for layout-aware salvage.
Attach measured pinout/topology before section salvage or external splice.
layout_reuse_boundaries.claim_boundary: Layout boundaries are conservative. Without front/back geometry and measured topology, the engine may recommend whole-board or connector reuse, but not board-section cutting or hidden-trace salvage.

reuse_splice_strategy.strategy_summary: treat the board as unknown until measured; readiness=visual_mapping_only

recipes.title: Measure first, then decide reuse versus harvest
splice_steps: Run no-short, voltage, current, and thermal checks.
acceptance: function is identified and measured
no hard safety blocker remains
prohibitions: Do not power or splice unknown boards from visual evidence alone.

allowed_actions: Capture markings, connector close-ups, and baseline continuity/no-short measurements.
Prepare candidate splice recipes but keep them gated.
reuse_splice_strategy.best_next_checkpoint: Attach measured connector pinout/topology evidence with no unknown pins.
reuse_splice_strategy.claim_boundary: Splice recipes are templates until connector pinout, voltage/current limits, and terminal outcome evidence are recorded.

blocking_gaps: Attach measured topology evidence for connector pinout, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal lanes by running the visual topology measurement queue.

arbitrary_board_trust_assessment.claim_boundary: Trust assessment is a readiness and gap report for arbitrary boards. It does not authorize power, splicing, sale, or production release; those remain gated by repair_authority and production_repair_authority.

tasks.prompt: Add an overview photo or annotated crop with normalized boxes for unmapped evidence items.

tasks.prompt: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
Map 40-pin GPIO header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map Ethernet RJ45: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map HDMI: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.

Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 40-pin GPIO header.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on Ethernet RJ45.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on HDMI.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-A port 1.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for 40-pin GPIO header before any external connection.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-A port 1 before any external connection.
Record connector ground to exposed ground. Confirm common ground reference.
Record current draw under current-limited supply. Record first-power current at a safe current limit.
Record input voltage and polarity. Measure supply rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Record logic high voltage. Measure the reused signal voltage domain.
Record power to ground no-short. Unpowered resistance check between supply and ground.
Record thermal behavior after first power. Record no abnormal heating after first power.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) ground reference continuity: 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) logic voltage domain: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Measure idle/high logic level before connecting to another controller or adapter.
Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) signal pin continuity map: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Map each reusable signal to a labeled pad, connector, or target net before splicing.
Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) supply voltage and polarity: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) supply-to-ground no-short: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3 vs 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.

Record Ethernet RJ45 (ethernet) high-speed/shield reference check: 1:BI_DA+, 2:BI_DA-, 3:BI_DB+, 4:BI_DC+, 5:BI_DC-, 6:BI_DB-, 7:BI_DD+, 8:BI_DD-. Treat high-speed pairs as reference-only unless connector, shield, and protection path are confirmed.
Record Ethernet RJ45 (ethernet) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record HDMI (hdmip) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record HDMI (hdmip) supply-to-ground no-short: 18:+5V vs 17:DDC_GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.
Add an overview or annotated photo that maps unmapped connector/component evidence into normalized board coordinates.

bench_protocol_pack.title: Unknown low-voltage board baseline protocol
required_equipment: current-limited bench supply or protected USB source
setup_controls: Keep unknown boards unpowered until no-short and polarity checks pass.

closure_lanes.objective: Turn visual/reference topology into measured connector, pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, and thermal evidence.

tasks.prompt: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
Map 40-pin GPIO header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map Ethernet RJ45: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map HDMI: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map USB-A port 1: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 40-pin GPIO header.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on Ethernet RJ45.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on HDMI.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-A port 1.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for 40-pin GPIO header before any external connection.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-A port 1 before any external connection.
Record connector ground to exposed ground. Confirm common ground reference.
Record current draw under current-limited supply. Record first-power current at a safe current limit.
Record input voltage and polarity. Measure supply rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Record logic high voltage. Measure the reused signal voltage domain.
Record power to ground no-short. Unpowered resistance check between supply and ground.
Record thermal behavior after first power. Record no abnormal heating after first power.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) ground reference continuity: 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) logic voltage domain: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Measure idle/high logic level before connecting to another controller or adapter.
Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) signal pin continuity map: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Map each reusable signal to a labeled pad, connector, or target net before splicing.
Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) supply voltage and polarity: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) supply-to-ground no-short: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3 vs 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.

Record Ethernet RJ45 (ethernet) high-speed/shield reference check: 1:BI_DA+, 2:BI_DA-, 3:BI_DB+, 4:BI_DC+, 5:BI_DC-, 6:BI_DB-, 7:BI_DD+, 8:BI_DD-. Treat high-speed pairs as reference-only unless connector, shield, and protection path are confirmed.
Record Ethernet RJ45 (ethernet) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record HDMI (hdmip) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record HDMI (hdmip) supply-to-ground no-short: 18:+5V vs 17:DDC_GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.
Add an overview or annotated photo that maps unmapped connector/component evidence into normalized board coordinates.

closure_lanes.title: Trusted bench evidence
closure_lanes.objective: Record trusted measurements with provenance so authority lanes can actually close.

tasks.prompt: Run the Unknown low-voltage board baseline protocol and attach trusted artifacts for: resistance, continuity, voltage, current, thermal, review.
Attach trusted continuity evidence with instrument id, calibration status, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI.
Attach trusted current evidence with instrument id, calibration status, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI.
Attach trusted resistance evidence with instrument id, calibration status, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI.
Attach trusted thermal evidence with instrument id, calibration status, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI.
Attach trusted voltage evidence with instrument id, calibration status, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI.
Map connector ground/reference and likely power pins.
Measure every obvious power rail or connector power pin to ground.
Observe thermal behavior during first-power.
Power only through a current limit after no-short passes.
Attach measured connector pinout/topology evidence with no unknown pins.
Attach measured topology evidence for connector pinout, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal lanes by running the visual topology measurement queue.
Attach trusted measurement artifacts for: continuity, current, resistance, thermal, voltage.
Record passing trusted continuity evidence.
Record passing trusted current evidence.
Record passing trusted resistance evidence.
Record passing trusted thermal evidence.
Record passing trusted voltage evidence.
Photograph front/back, labels, connectors, and damage before testing.

closure_lanes.title: Terminal functional outcome
closure_lanes.objective: Prove the scoped reused function worked after the evidence-gated build or repair trial.

tasks.prompt: Record terminal build/repair/reuse outcome.
Record terminal outcome with selected resources used, first-power result, thermal result, output_function_verified, evidence URI, cost, value, time, and deviations.

closure_lanes.title: Release package
closure_lanes.objective: Package the scoped claim so the project can show exactly what is proven and what remains excluded.

active_evidence_closure_plan.claim_boundary: This plan ranks evidence to collect next. It does not authorize power, repair, splice, sale, or production release until measured topology, trusted measurements, terminal outcome, and release artifacts pass.

hardware_plan_summary.claim_boundary: Hardware plan is advisory until repair authority, resource evidence gates, and first-power measurements close.

resource_interpretation.core_rule: salvage, owned inventory, procurement, and designed blocks are scored as resources; the resource pool changes, not the engine.
resource_interpretation.constrained: Prefer already-owned or salvaged resources and surface evidence gates before reuse.
resource_interpretation.open_procurement: Prefer reliable procurable modules when they beat uncertain reuse.
resource_interpretation.hybrid: Reuse proven resources and buy only the gaps.

wiring_steps: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos
power the reused block from a current-limited supply at the lowest plausible rated voltage
verify the block output function before connecting it to any other module
mount the new build so wires have strain relief and cannot short
record a first-power test, thermal check, and final output function
mechanical_steps: keep strain relief on harvested wires and connectors
mount moving parts so wires cannot touch blades, gears, belts, rails, or hot surfaces
label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification
do_not_connect_until: rail voltage is measured under current limit
battery or mains hazards are cleared

stop_conditions: input voltage or polarity cannot be identified
load current exceeds the wire, connector, switch, driver, or supply rating
module overheats, smells, smokes, or pulls unexpected current
battery chemistry/protection is unknown or the pack is damaged

lanes.title: Layout geometry anchors
lanes.request_prompt: Add an overview photo or annotated crop with normalized boxes for unmapped evidence items.

lanes.title: Cross-view confirmation
lanes.request_prompt: Recapture the main components/connectors from another angle so single-photo claims can be cross-checked.

lanes.title: Backside or hidden-area context
lanes.request_prompt: If accessible, capture the backside, solder side, cable underside, or hidden area so hidden routes and damage are not silently assumed.

capture_coverage.claim_boundary: Capture coverage grades visual observability only; it cannot prove pinout, nets, voltage, or safe reuse.

reconstruction_summary.source_policy: Repeated visual observations raise candidate confidence but do not prove pinout, nets, voltage, or safe reuse.

measurement_queue.prompt: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
Add an overview or annotated photo that maps unmapped connector/component evidence into normalized board coordinates.
Map 40-pin GPIO header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 40-pin GPIO header.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for 40-pin GPIO header before any external connection.
Map Ethernet RJ45: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on Ethernet RJ45.
Map HDMI: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on HDMI.
Map USB-A port 1: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-A port 1.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-A port 1 before any external connection.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-A port 1 to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
Map USB-A port 2: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-A port 2.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-A port 2 before any external connection.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-A port 2 to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
Map USB-C Power In: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-C Power In.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-C Power In before any external connection.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-C Power In to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
Map 3.5mm audio jack: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 3.5mm audio jack.

blocked_claims.reason: Visible adjacency and approximate boxes do not prove hidden copper, vias, inner layers, or backside routing.

items.summary: Visual board candidates exist without measured topology.
evidence: Vision evidence can propose components and connectors but cannot prove pinout.
items.resolution: Capture measured connector pinout, no-short, ground, and voltage evidence.

steps.action: Attach measured connector pinout/topology evidence with no unknown pins.
steps.expected_result: Every used connector pin has a measured role and voltage/domain where relevant.
steps.fail_branch: Measured pinout is complete.

steps.action: Record passing trusted resistance evidence.
steps.expected_result: No power rail reads as a dead short to ground.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing resistance evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted continuity evidence.
steps.expected_result: Ground/reference continuity is stable.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing continuity evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted voltage evidence.
steps.expected_result: Voltage and polarity match the measured low-voltage domain.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing voltage evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted current evidence.
steps.expected_result: Current draw remains within the configured current limit.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing current evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted thermal evidence.
steps.expected_result: Thermal behavior remains normal through the function test.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing thermal evidence.

steps.action: Record successful first power, thermal behavior, and output_function_verified=true.
steps.expected_result: Successful output function, first power, and thermal outcome are recorded.
steps.fail_branch: Terminal successful outcome is recorded.

steps.action: Record passing ground/reference continuity and connector continuity evidence.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing current draw/current-limit evidence during first power.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing voltage, polarity, and logic-level evidence under current limit.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Measure unpowered resistance between every power rail and ground.
steps.expected_result: No rail reads as a dead short unless the design explicitly expects it.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; isolate the shorted rail before power.

steps.action: Power through a current limit and record input voltage, rail voltage, and polarity.
steps.expected_result: Rails match the measured or expected low-voltage domain.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; inspect regulator/input protection path.

steps.action: Record thermal behavior during first-power and function test.
steps.expected_result: No abnormal hot spot, smoke, odor, or thermal runaway.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; isolate load, regulator, or shorted component.

bench_protocol_pack.title: Unknown low-voltage board baseline protocol
required_equipment: current-limited bench supply or protected USB source
setup_controls: Keep unknown boards unpowered until no-short and polarity checks pass.
Use current limits for every first-power or load test.
Record instrument, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI for each passing measurement.
Stop on smoke, odor, abnormal heat, unexpected current, shorted rail, or unstable output.
For BCM2711, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For SBC, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For RASPBERRY PI, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.

steps.action: Photograph front/back, labels, connectors, and damage before testing.
steps.expected_result: Visual record is complete.

steps.action: Measure every obvious power rail or connector power pin to ground.
steps.expected_result: No dead short.

steps.action: Map connector ground/reference and likely power pins.
steps.expected_result: Baseline connector roles are known.

steps.action: Power only through a current limit after no-short passes.
steps.expected_result: Voltage/current behavior is bounded.

steps.action: Observe thermal behavior during first-power.
steps.expected_result: No abnormal hot spot.

acceptance: Board identity is narrowed to a reusable function.
No-short, pinout, voltage/current, and thermal evidence pass.
Terminal function outcome is recorded before release.

release_artifacts_required: production release manifest for any release claim
bench_protocol_pack.release_boundary: This pack defines what must be proven for a scoped low-voltage release. It cannot authorize battery, mains, high-voltage, laser, or unbounded reuse without a separate specialist authority.

candidates.label: USB/serial bridge, connector, boot, or logic-level fault
diagnostic_actions: Run logic-level, shared-ground, loopback, and safe serial-capture steps.

candidates.label: Sensor front-end, bus, pullup, or connector fault
diagnostic_actions: Verify supply, pullups, bus idle, low-speed scan, and connector continuity.

candidates.label: Display/UI pin map, current limit, or connector fault
diagnostic_actions: Map pins, verify current limits, and test through protected controller I/O.

candidates.label: Audio load, amplifier, connector, or protection fault
diagnostic_actions: Verify load impedance, idle current, low-level output, and thermal behavior.

salvage_value_decision.rationale: Visual evidence can identify candidates, but value depends on measured function.
salvage_value_decision.recommended_exit: Collect baseline measurements before spending parts or time.
salvage_value_decision.claim_boundary: Economic estimate is heuristic until a successful function outcome is recorded.

Verify RASPBERRY PI marking, package/pinout, and board variant before relying on catalog specs: type, requires.

part_grounding.claim_boundary: Part markings and catalog knowledge can ground likely function and tests, but they do not prove wiring, pinout, board variant, or safe power without measurements.

salvage_items.recommended_handling: Treat as supporting inventory; value depends on board-level function proof.
suggested_uses: parts evidence for a future build

salvage_items.recommended_handling: Treat as supporting inventory; value depends on board-level function proof.
suggested_uses: parts evidence for a future build

salvage_items.label: 3.5mm audio jack
salvage_items.recommended_handling: Reuse only after impedance, idle-current, and low-level output checks.

salvage_items.label: 40-pin GPIO header
salvage_items.recommended_handling: Harvest or reuse as an entry point after continuity and pin-label confirmation.

salvage_items.label: Ethernet RJ45
salvage_items.recommended_handling: Harvest or reuse as an entry point after continuity and pin-label confirmation.

salvage_items.label: HDMI
salvage_items.recommended_handling: Harvest or reuse as an entry point after continuity and pin-label confirmation.

salvage_items.label: USB-A port 1
salvage_items.recommended_handling: Harvest or reuse as an entry point after continuity and pin-label confirmation.

salvage_items.label: USB-A port 2
salvage_items.recommended_handling: Harvest or reuse as an entry point after continuity and pin-label confirmation.

salvage_items.label: USB-C Power In
salvage_items.recommended_handling: Reuse as a power stage only after no-short, voltage, load-current, and thermal checks.

salvage_items.label: BCM2711 grounded by visible marking
salvage_items.recommended_handling: Use catalog grounding for single-board computer; verify board-specific pinout, package, and measurements before reuse.
suggested_uses: parts evidence for a future build

allowed_layout_actions: whole-board reuse after authority gates close
connector-based harness reuse after measured pinout
label and fixture known connector entry points
prohibited_layout_actions: board-section cutting from visual evidence alone
hidden-trace splice or copper cut without backside/topology proof
missing_layout_evidence: Attach additional angle/side/closeup photos before any section salvage or trace-cut claim.
Attach component/connector geometry or annotated regions for layout-aware salvage.
Attach measured pinout/topology before section salvage or external splice.
layout_reuse_boundaries.claim_boundary: Layout boundaries are conservative. Without front/back geometry and measured topology, the engine may recommend whole-board or connector reuse, but not board-section cutting or hidden-trace salvage.

reuse_splice_strategy.strategy_summary: treat the board as unknown until measured; readiness=visual_mapping_only

recipes.title: Measure first, then decide reuse versus harvest
splice_steps: Run no-short, voltage, current, and thermal checks.
acceptance: function is identified and measured
no hard safety blocker remains
prohibitions: Do not power or splice unknown boards from visual evidence alone.

allowed_actions: Capture markings, connector close-ups, and baseline continuity/no-short measurements.
Prepare candidate splice recipes but keep them gated.
reuse_splice_strategy.best_next_checkpoint: Attach measured connector pinout/topology evidence with no unknown pins.
reuse_splice_strategy.claim_boundary: Splice recipes are templates until connector pinout, voltage/current limits, and terminal outcome evidence are recorded.

blocking_gaps: Attach measured topology evidence for connector pinout, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal lanes by running the visual topology measurement queue.
Attach trusted measurement artifacts for: continuity, current, resistance, thermal, voltage.
Record terminal build/repair/reuse outcome.
Attach production_release or release_manifest.

arbitrary_board_trust_assessment.claim_boundary: Trust assessment is a readiness and gap report for arbitrary boards. It does not authorize power, splicing, sale, or production release; those remain gated by repair_authority and production_repair_authority.

candidates.label: USB/serial bridge, connector, boot, or logic-level fault
diagnostic_actions: Run logic-level, shared-ground, loopback, and safe serial-capture steps.

candidates.label: Sensor front-end, bus, pullup, or connector fault
diagnostic_actions: Verify supply, pullups, bus idle, low-speed scan, and connector continuity.

candidates.label: Display/UI pin map, current limit, or connector fault
diagnostic_actions: Map pins, verify current limits, and test through protected controller I/O.

candidates.label: Audio load, amplifier, connector, or protection fault
diagnostic_actions: Verify load impedance, idle current, low-level output, and thermal behavior.

salvage_value_decision.rationale: Visual evidence can identify candidates, but value depends on measured function.
salvage_value_decision.recommended_exit: Collect baseline measurements before spending parts or time.
salvage_value_decision.claim_boundary: Economic estimate is heuristic until a successful function outcome is recorded.

allowed_layout_actions: whole-board reuse after authority gates close
connector-based harness reuse after measured pinout
label and fixture known connector entry points
prohibited_layout_actions: board-section cutting from visual evidence alone
hidden-trace splice or copper cut without backside/topology proof
missing_layout_evidence: Attach additional angle/side/closeup photos before any section salvage or trace-cut claim.
Attach component/connector geometry or annotated regions for layout-aware salvage.
Attach measured pinout/topology before section salvage or external splice.
layout_reuse_boundaries.claim_boundary: Layout boundaries are conservative. Without front/back geometry and measured topology, the engine may recommend whole-board or connector reuse, but not board-section cutting or hidden-trace salvage.

reuse_splice_strategy.strategy_summary: treat the board as unknown until measured; readiness=visual_mapping_only

recipes.title: Measure first, then decide reuse versus harvest
splice_steps: Run no-short, voltage, current, and thermal checks.
acceptance: function is identified and measured
no hard safety blocker remains
prohibitions: Do not power or splice unknown boards from visual evidence alone.

Prepare candidate splice recipes but keep them gated.

reuse_splice_strategy.claim_boundary: Splice recipes are templates until connector pinout, voltage/current limits, and terminal outcome evidence are recorded.

closure_lanes.title: Cross-view visual coverage

tasks.prompt: Add an overview photo or annotated crop with normalized boxes for unmapped evidence items.
Add more board photos from different distances or angles.
Capture a wider photo that includes readable markings and their surrounding component context.
Capture readable close-ups of every IC marking, connector label, regulator marking, crystal, and silkscreen model text.

tasks.prompt: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
Map 40-pin GPIO header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map Ethernet RJ45: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map HDMI: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map USB-A port 1: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 40-pin GPIO header.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on Ethernet RJ45.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on HDMI.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-A port 1.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for 40-pin GPIO header before any external connection.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-A port 1 before any external connection.
Record connector ground to exposed ground. Confirm common ground reference.
Record current draw under current-limited supply. Record first-power current at a safe current limit.
Record input voltage and polarity. Measure supply rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Record logic high voltage. Measure the reused signal voltage domain.
Record power to ground no-short. Unpowered resistance check between supply and ground.
Record thermal behavior after first power. Record no abnormal heating after first power.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) ground reference continuity: 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) logic voltage domain: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Measure idle/high logic level before connecting to another controller or adapter.
Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) signal pin continuity map: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Map each reusable signal to a labeled pad, connector, or target net before splicing.
Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) supply voltage and polarity: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) supply-to-ground no-short: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3 vs 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.

Record Ethernet RJ45 (ethernet) high-speed/shield reference check: 1:BI_DA+, 2:BI_DA-, 3:BI_DB+, 4:BI_DC+, 5:BI_DC-, 6:BI_DB-, 7:BI_DD+, 8:BI_DD-. Treat high-speed pairs as reference-only unless connector, shield, and protection path are confirmed.
Record Ethernet RJ45 (ethernet) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record HDMI (hdmip) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record HDMI (hdmip) supply-to-ground no-short: 18:+5V vs 17:DDC_GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.
Add an overview or annotated photo that maps unmapped connector/component evidence into normalized board coordinates.

candidates.label: Sensor front-end, bus, pullup, or connector fault

candidates.label: Display/UI pin map, current limit, or connector fault

candidates.label: Audio load, amplifier, connector, or protection fault
diagnostic_actions: Verify load impedance, idle current, low-level output, and thermal behavior.

instructions: Measure resistance between supply and ground before applying power.

instructions: Record the terminal outcome with output_function_verified=true after the build demonstrates its target function.

closure_lanes.title: Cross-view visual coverage
closure_lanes.objective: Get enough non-rigid photo observations to cross-check markings, connectors, damage, and placement.

tasks.prompt: Add an overview photo or annotated crop with normalized boxes for unmapped evidence items.
Add more board photos from different distances or angles.
Capture a wider photo that includes readable markings and their surrounding component context.
Capture readable close-ups of every IC marking, connector label, regulator marking, crystal, and silkscreen model text.
Capture several non-identical board observations: whole-board overview with scale, angled view, connector edge, and any backside/hidden area that exists.
If accessible, capture the backside, solder side, cable underside, or hidden area so hidden routes and damage are not silently assumed.
Recapture the main components/connectors from another angle so single-photo claims can be cross-checked.
Capture a damage and safety review pass: battery areas, hot spots, corrosion, burns, cracked parts, bodge wires, and power input path.

closure_lanes.title: Part and product identity
closure_lanes.objective: Ground board function claims in visible markings, known packages, public pinouts, or datasheets.

closure_lanes.title: Measured topology and pinout
closure_lanes.objective: Turn visual/reference topology into measured connector, pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, and thermal evidence.

tasks.prompt: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
Map 40-pin GPIO header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map Ethernet RJ45: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map HDMI: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map USB-A port 1: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 40-pin GPIO header.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on Ethernet RJ45.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on HDMI.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-A port 1.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for 40-pin GPIO header before any external connection.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-A port 1 before any external connection.
Record connector ground to exposed ground. Confirm common ground reference.
Record current draw under current-limited supply. Record first-power current at a safe current limit.
Record input voltage and polarity. Measure supply rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Record logic high voltage. Measure the reused signal voltage domain.
Record power to ground no-short. Unpowered resistance check between supply and ground.
Record thermal behavior after first power. Record no abnormal heating after first power.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) ground reference continuity: 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) logic voltage domain: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Measure idle/high logic level before connecting to another controller or adapter.
Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) signal pin continuity map: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Map each reusable signal to a labeled pad, connector, or target net before splicing.
Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) supply voltage and polarity: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.

Record 40-pin GPIO header (gpio_header) supply-to-ground no-short: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3 vs 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.

Record Ethernet RJ45 (ethernet) high-speed/shield reference check: 1:BI_DA+, 2:BI_DA-, 3:BI_DB+, 4:BI_DC+, 5:BI_DC-, 6:BI_DB-, 7:BI_DD+, 8:BI_DD-. Treat high-speed pairs as reference-only unless connector, shield, and protection path are confirmed.
Record Ethernet RJ45 (ethernet) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.

active_evidence_closure_plan.claim_boundary: This plan ranks evidence to collect next. It does not authorize power, repair, splice, sale, or production release until measured topology, trusted measurements, terminal outcome, and release artifacts pass.

hardware_plan_summary.claim_boundary: Hardware plan is advisory until repair authority, resource evidence gates, and first-power measurements close.

resource_interpretation.core_rule: salvage, owned inventory, procurement, and designed blocks are scored as resources; the resource pool changes, not the engine.
resource_interpretation.constrained: Prefer already-owned or salvaged resources and surface evidence gates before reuse.
resource_interpretation.open_procurement: Prefer reliable procurable modules when they beat uncertain reuse.
resource_interpretation.hybrid: Reuse proven resources and buy only the gaps.

wiring_steps: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos
power the reused block from a current-limited supply at the lowest plausible rated voltage
verify the block output function before connecting it to any other module
mount the new build so wires have strain relief and cannot short
record a first-power test, thermal check, and final output function
mechanical_steps: keep strain relief on harvested wires and connectors
mount moving parts so wires cannot touch blades, gears, belts, rails, or hot surfaces
label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification
do_not_connect_until: rail voltage is measured under current limit
battery or mains hazards are cleared

operational_call.summary: Capture measured topology or supply a versioned simulation netlist.
operational_call.why: The module-level test suite exists, but a versioned netlist is required before deterministic simulation.
tools: Qwen vision pass for connector candidates
procedure: Use Qwen/vision to identify candidate connectors, rails, labels, and likely pin groups.
Measure ground continuity and power-to-ground no-short.
Measure rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Submit topology_evidence.v1 or a versioned netlist and rerun the field operator.
operational_call.on_pass: Compiler should emit a netlist or bounded envelope.
operational_call.on_fail: Continue topology capture; do not power/splice without measured topology.

model_assignments.authority_boundary: Qwen can propose where to probe; it cannot verify voltage, current, no-short, or safety.

model_assignments.call: prioritize measurements needed to convert candidates into topology evidence
model_assignments.authority_boundary: DeepSeek can structure notes, explain failures, and suggest next measurements; deterministic gates decide trust.

model_assignments.call: rerun topology compiler, power-tree simulation, and design test kit after new evidence
model_assignments.authority_boundary: Only measured evidence plus deterministic gates can advance power/splice/release state.

decision_inputs.release_reason: The module-level test suite exists, but a versioned netlist is required before deterministic simulation.

field_agent.claim_boundary: The field operator can choose photos, measurements, stop calls, and redesign tasks. Qwen/DeepSeek outputs are advisory field observations until measured evidence and deterministic gates confirm them. The field operator cannot independently authorize high-risk power, splice, or production release.
